title: AR/VR + metaverse + crypto/gaming thesis (Oct 2021)
html:
TL;DR

  • Four-line thesis sketch four-days after Facebook's Oct-28-2021 rebrand to Meta: Yahir flags AR/VR + metaverse, crypto-gaming intersection, automation-taking-over, AI — the four secular bets he'd refine over the next five years.
  • First explicit metaverse mention in vault, captured the literal week the term went mainstream — Yahir is on it inside 96 hours of the Facebook→Meta announcement.
  • "Crypto/Gaming intersection" is the proto-thesis that resurfaces in 2024–2025 as PHIB memecoin work and Swipr/VRTL peer-wager experiments — the gaming-as-financial-rails frame is already here at 18.
  • "Automation taking over everything" + "AI" listed as separate items — Yahir distinguishes general automation from AI specifically, four-plus years before his own AI search work makes him a participant.
  • Sits in the dense October-2021 cluster (three things manifest, Tesla aspiration, Tesla vs Edison, Buffett vs Bezos) — Yahir is in a sustained life-frame + macro-thesis sprint that month.

Note

Four lines. Yahir at 18, freshman at OSU, reading the Meta rebrand and translating it into a personal watchlist. The order of the four — AR/VR first, crypto-gaming second, automation third, AI fourth — is the inverse of how the next five years would actually rank them in market relevance, which makes the note a real-time snapshot of how a smart 18-year-old prioritized in late 2021 vs. how the decade actually played out. Worth re-reading against the CEOs need engineering note eight days later, where Yahir extends the same thesis into a labor-market frame.

Connections

  • metaverse — first-mention
  • phib · swipr · vrtl bet — crypto-gaming intersection downstream
  • the austin thesis — AI thread Yahir later joins
  • 2021 11 05 ceos of the future need engineering — companion piece
  • post pandemic future thinking · disruption
